architecture_amendment_rule: A downstream change requires a reviewed amendment naming superseded rules and preserving audit history.

decisions.title: Layer and dependency direction
decisions.decision: Freeze domain, runtime, persistence, adapters, routes, projections, and web as separate layers. Domain remains pure; runtime orchestrates ports; persistence owns SQL and transactions; routes own auth/scope/validation; web remains read-only runtime presentation.
decisions.rationale: This prevents database, wall-clock, HTTP, and UI concerns from becoming physical-State semantics while retaining useful pure builders.
rejected_alternatives: Fastify routes compute or advance State
Domain builders read Postgres, environment, wall clock, filesystem, network, or random UUID
invariants: domain has no infrastructure dependency
routes do not own generated-object writes
web cannot write State, Forecast, Scenario result, Checkpoint, or Activation

decisions.title: Shared runtime core and mode adapters
decisions.decision: Replay, Shadow-online, Controlled Field, and future Production share one semantic core. Only clock, ingress, scheduler, execution feedback, availability, and deployment vary through adapters.
decisions.rationale: Separate semantic implementations would make replay unable to validate online behavior and would recreate demo/runtime divergence.
rejected_alternatives: One semantic runtime per mode
Replay-only file persistence or mode-specific State semantics
invariants: same inputs and config yield the same semantic payload in every mode
Replay uses explicit time and no-future-leakage
Shadow-online has no AO-ACT creation authority

decisions.title: Canonical persistence and envelope profiles
decisions.decision: Postgres facts remain the only canonical append-only store. Every object declares record class, lineage membership, envelope profile and transaction families. LINEAGE requires lineage_id and revision_id; INITIAL revision identity does not require a revision-run object.
decisions.rationale: Record authority, lineage membership and revision-run lifecycle are separate dimensions. This permits deterministic initial lineage identity while retaining non-lineage bootstrap and failure audit.
rejected_alternatives: One shared envelope requiring lineage and revision-run references for every record
Infer lineage membership from record class
Require a fabricated revision run for INITIAL revision identity
invariants: canonical facts never update
indexes may upsert and must be rebuildable
record class does not imply lineage membership
INITIAL revision_id does not imply twin_revision_run_v1
non-lineage audit may exist before bootstrap, config, lineage, or checkpoint

decisions.title: State object granularity and atomicity
decisions.decision: A valid State advance atomically appends transition, assimilation update and posterior State. BOOTSTRAP embeds its configured prior in the transition, has no previous posterior, and does not create an independent prior object.
decisions.rationale: The embedded prior keeps bootstrap computation explicit without creating a dangling reference or falsely treating the prior as current State.
rejected_alternatives: Independent canonical prior State
Latest sensor reading directly becomes root-zone State
Dangling bootstrap_prior_ref without a canonical object
invariants: prior is not canonical current State
posterior is canonical State
sensor reading is Evidence, never direct State
transition, update, and posterior commit atomically
BOOTSTRAP previous_posterior_ref is null
assimilation is not calibration

decisions.title: Tick claim, compute, and outcome transactions
decisions.decision: A tick has Claim, Compute and Commit. A0 creates the first lineage, posterior, BLOCKED Forecast and checkpoint atomically; A1 and A2 continue an existing lineage. Forecast FAILED remains operational transaction F.
decisions.rationale: A0 closes the first-tick gap while preserving the existing distinction between valid State progress, blocked Forecast prerequisites and failed computation.
rejected_alternatives: Persist FAILED Forecast inside State Tick Commit
Create first State outside transaction A
Require a fake previous posterior or checkpoint
invariants: A0 appends exactly nine canonical facts
A0 Forecast is BLOCKED with zero points and reason codes
A1 COMPLETED has a 72-point Forecast
A2 BLOCKED has a zero-point Forecast and COMPLETED_WITH_LIMITATIONS tick
FAILED creates no canonical tick or checkpoint
frozen evidence excludes later arrivals

decisions.title: Checkpoint, lease, fencing, restart, and backfill
decisions.decision: Each scope has one fenced writer. A0 inserts the INITIAL checkpoint with null previous_checkpoint_ref after new-key null-CAS checks. A1/A2 append continuation checkpoints. Failed A transactions leave all A pointers unchanged.
decisions.rationale: The first checkpoint cannot reference a predecessor, while fencing, canonical INITIAL uniqueness and expected-null CAS prevent competing bootstrap histories.
rejected_alternatives: Lease without fencing
Fabricated previous checkpoint
Pointer-only check that permits a second canonical INITIAL lineage
invariants: one writer per tenant/project/group/field/season/zone
old fencing token cannot commit
INITIAL previous_checkpoint_ref is null
projection loss cannot authorize a second INITIAL lineage
failure never advances checkpoint

decisions.title: Idempotency and deterministic identity
decisions.decision: Every semantic object has deterministic identity. A0 additionally has a deterministic nine-object aggregate identity and independently computed aggregate hash. Existing-key idempotency is resolved before new-key null-CAS checks.
decisions.rationale: A successful A0 necessarily makes pointers non-null; checking idempotency first permits correct same-input replay without weakening new-key bootstrap preconditions.
rejected_alternatives: Random UUID or fact_id as semantic identity
Overwrite on idempotency conflict
Run null-CAS before checking an existing A0 idempotency key
invariants: same semantic A0 returns the same complete nine-object record set
fact_id, audit timestamps, process and branch data are excluded from determinism hash
same key different hash is IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT
declared hash mismatch is SEMANTIC_HASH_MISMATCH
different canonical INITIAL lineage is INITIAL_LINEAGE_CONFLICT

decisions.title: Initial activation and late-Evidence revision lifecycle
decisions.decision: NULL_TO_INITIAL activation is authorized by successful A0 plus an INITIAL lineage declaration. Replacing an existing active lineage remains authorized only by E3 promotion after revision completion and validation.
decisions.rationale: Initial creation is not promotion. Keeping the authorities distinct avoids a fictitious promotion while preserving strict append-only authority for all later active-lineage replacement.
rejected_alternatives: Create a fake initial promotion record
Allow active-to-active switch without E3 promotion
Treat INITIAL lineage as late-Evidence revision candidate
invariants: INITIAL declaration plus A0 is null-to-initial authority
candidate declaration is not activation
revision progress is append-only
active-to-active promotion is atomic
superseded history remains queryable

decisions.title: Forecast result and failure separation
decisions.decision: twin_forecast_run_v1 permits COMPLETED with exactly 72 points or BLOCKED with zero points and reason_codes. FAILED is forbidden on the lineage Forecast object and uses non-lineage twin_forecast_failure_v1 in transaction F.
decisions.rationale: BLOCKED describes a valid posterior with an unavailable downstream Forecast prerequisite. FAILED describes failed computation and cannot advance checkpoint.
rejected_alternatives: Treat BLOCKED and FAILED as the same status
Persist FAILED twin_forecast_run_v1 inside State Tick Commit
invariants: COMPLETED advances checkpoint and successful Forecast index
BLOCKED advances checkpoint but not successful Forecast index
FAILED advances neither tick nor checkpoint
t0 is not a Forecast point

decisions.title: Scenario source eligibility
decisions.decision: Scenario is a separate aggregate and may use only a committed COMPLETED Forecast with exactly 72 points and horizons 1 through 72. BLOCKED and FAILED outcomes are ineligible.
decisions.rationale: Comparable Scenario trajectories require a complete forecast horizon.
rejected_alternatives: Generate Scenario from a zero-point BLOCKED Forecast
Generate Scenario from failure diagnostics
invariants: fixed five Gate-A options
Scenario is not Recommendation
Scenario commit is separate
BLOCKED or FAILED Forecast cannot source Scenario

decisions.title: Residual and calibration separation
decisions.decision: Assimilation residual belongs to the current update. Forecast residual compares later Evidence with a historical COMPLETED Forecast point and never mutates the Forecast.
decisions.rationale: The two residuals have different temporal and governance roles; conflation would corrupt history and silently change models.
rejected_alternatives: One residual object for assimilation and forecast verification
Residual directly changes active model or historical forecast
invariants: assimilation residual is not calibration
forecast residual does not mutate Forecast
candidate is not active model

decisions.title: Model/config binding and activation
decisions.decision: Each tick resolves one immutable config before compute. Candidate, shadow evaluation, approval and activation are separate append-only transitions. Activation changes only at a tick boundary.
decisions.rationale: A tick must not mix versions, and mutable active pointers need append-only authority and later consumption proof.
rejected_alternatives: Read active parameters repeatedly during a tick
Candidate self-activation or rollback by deleting history
invariants: config is frozen before compute
activation changes only at tick boundary
later outputs prove active-config consumption
no automatic activation

decisions.title: Read API and human write API separation
decisions.decision: Runtime Operator APIs remain read-only under the field-scoped family. Generated objects have no public write endpoint. Human decision and action writes remain separately governed.
decisions.rationale: Extending the existing field-scoped family avoids a parallel namespace and prevents browser submission of generated objects.
rejected_alternatives: New parallel Operator runtime namespace
Public HTTP tick or generated-object write endpoints
invariants: Operator Runtime APIs are read-only
manual tick begins as application service, CLI/manual runner, and scheduler adapter
human writes remain separately governed

decisions.title: Legacy compatibility and migration
decisions.decision: Legacy paths remain read-only compatibility surfaces with no new MCFT capability or dual canonical write. Deletion requires caller migration and explicit approval.
decisions.rationale: Active callers and read contracts make immediate deletion unsafe, while continued feature growth would create dual mainlines.
rejected_alternatives: Delete legacy routes in DT-02
Dual-write legacy and new canonical State or Scenario
invariants: legacy reads remain available
legacy paths gain no new capability
no dual canonical write
replacement is proven before deletion

decisions.title: Runtime security and write authority
decisions.decision: Write authority is object and variant specific. Runtime Orchestrator owns A0 INITIAL lineage declaration and State Tick outcomes; Revision Service owns E1/E2; Promotion Service owns E3; failure audit and Action Feedback remain separately governed.
decisions.rationale: Variant-specific authority permits the initial lineage to be created without granting the Runtime Orchestrator general revision-promotion authority.
rejected_alternatives: Revision Service fabricates INITIAL lineage through E1
Runtime Orchestrator gains active-to-active promotion authority
Require formal Acceptance before trustworthy executed Evidence
invariants: writer authority is conditional by object and operation variant
checkpoint writes are fenced
initial declaration is not revision promotion
decision, approval, execution, and validation remain separate
Acceptance is not prerequisite for all trustworthy executed Evidence

decisions.title: Runtime health, failure audit, and claim boundaries
decisions.decision: Attempt, Health and Forecast failure are non-lineage operational audit. A failed A0 has zero A0 facts, projections and pointer changes, but may append a separately transacted F audit that never claims committed State success.
decisions.rationale: Operational failure remains auditable without being miscounted as partial bootstrap success or physical-State history.
rejected_alternatives: Require active lineage before recording failure audit
Count an F audit record as an A0 partial canonical write
Represent Forecast failure as lineage Forecast
invariants: attempt is not State history
health is not physical State
failure audit needs no lineage
failed A0 changes no A0 pointer
stale writer cannot report committed success
